data.description: Starting at 1st level, your patron bestows you the ability to project beguiling and fearsome presence as an action. You can cause each creature in a 10-foot cube originating from you to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell DC. The creatures that fail this save are charmed or frightened by you (your choice) until the end of your next turn. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.
A realm of twisted logic and maddening paradoxes, where the laws of reality are constantly shifting.
A powerful and ancient being from the Otherworld, a Great One wields immense magical power and often seeks to guide or control mortal heroes.

can in a of in at leveI,you vanish puff mist response harm. you damage, can to When take DARK ONE'S BLESSING your up 60 and to invisible teleport to feet when you take damage. reaction turn at levei, you a creature can Vou an you see. remain invisible to points, gain until start the ofyour turn until attack cast spell.

Starting at 6th level, you can call on your patron to alter fate in your favor. When making a saving throw or ability check, you can use a feature to add advantage to the roll. Beginning at 10th level, your patron teaches you how to turn mind-affecting magic against your enemies. You are immune to charm effects directed at you, and when another creature attempts to charm you as a reaction, that creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell DC or be charmed by you for 1 minute until the creature takes damage.

Starting 14th level: As an action, you can plunge a creature into the realm of illusory planes at level {level}, and choose a creature within 60 feet that you can see. The creature must make a Wisdom saving throw against your warlock save DC. On a failed save, it is charmed and frightened by you and hurtles through a nightmare landscape. At the end of your turn, the target returns to the space previously occupied or the nearest unoccupied space. If the target is not a fiend, it takes 10d10 psychic damage from this horrific experience. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

This spell creates an illusion of a misty otherworldly realm. Until the spell ends, a creature that you choose within range thinks it lost its way in this misty realm and can't find its way back to where it started from until the illusion ends. The creature can see and hear itself, but everything else around it appears as if it were in a misty dreamland. You can end the spell early by concentrating on it for at least 1 minute. If the target takes damage or is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Wisdom saving throw to resist the effect, the illusion ends for it.
A fiend is an Otherworldly Patron that represents chaotic evil and manifests as a demonic creature.

Your patron is mysterious whose nature is utterly. It comes from the fabric reality, a foreign realm, or could be beyond the Far Realm, it might only legends in elder gods of motives and knowledge so incomprehensible to mortals that even the greatest libraries hold pale comparison to the secrets it holds. The Great One be the vast existence, of existence in all its ancient and immense secrets might be unaware your entire ignorance or indifferent to you but allow you to learn magic from it. Entities this include called Ghallanadar, God Tharizdun, Chained Dendar, Night Serpent Zargon, Retilter, Cthullu, and unfathomable beings.
